observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU officials strictly adhere and comply with the basic rules in the utilization of fuel of government vehicles as provided for in Section V of COA Circular No.  75-6    November 7, 1975.
observations.managementAction: The municipality failed to submit monthly fuel consumption report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the PRAISE Committee submits the employees’ individual accomplishment/appraisal or evaluation reports or employees’ contribution in compliance with the provisions of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2001.  Likewise, Municipality’s PRAISE Committee must have a written established guidelines in assessing the performance and conduct of each employee.

We recommended that management (i) minimize or stop the practice of serving meals and snacks for entertaining guests or visitors in the normal course of conducting official functions and during ordinary staff meetings or conferences in order to prevent the incurrence of irregular an unnecessary expenditures and wastage of government funds;
 (ii) ensure that expenses to be reimbursed pass the “public purpose test” by requiring that the basic supporting documents for government expenditures are attached to the claims;
We recommended that the agency develop and maintain a Gender and Development data base for gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data for planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended and the management agreed that the Municipal Accountant prepare and disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements for CY 2022 and years thereafter the relevant disclosures on the LDRRMF required under COA Circular No. 2012-002 for a fairer presentation of accounts under the fund.

We recommended and the management agreed that the:
Municipal Accountant and BAC Committee submit the BAC Resolution for adopting the use of emergency procurement as an alternative mode of procurement, list of beneficiaries signifying their receipt of good/supplies, and other supporting documents required under GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and PD No. 1445, otherwise, submit justification on the non-submission of such documents; and
Henceforth, Municipal Accountant be more vigilant in the processing of claims on LDRRMF by requiring compliance in the submission of complete documentary requirements and ensure that the documents are properly authorized/approved before effecting payment to establish validity and propriety of the transactions and to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended and the management agreed to enact a resolution to open a separate depository account for the 20% DF, transfer the unspent fund and regularly transfer the 20% IRA from the General Fund account to this account pursuant to the Section 313 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended and the management agreed that necessary action on the settlement of the Notices of Suspensions and Notices of Charges as of   December 31, 2021 in the total amount of ₱3.882 million will be done.

We recommended that an Inspection Committee should be constituted to determine and classify the unserviceable properties. The valueless should be destroyed in the presence of the Auditor under the supervision of the proper committee or if found valuable, it should be disposed of on the manner provided under Section 379 of R. A. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The designated Property Custodian started inventory of unserviceable properties.

There were partial remittances of collections, but huge amount remained unremitted.

Reiterated in this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:

The Municipal Accountant adjust the previously recorded RPT and SET Receivable based on the Certified List of Delinquent Accounts, to correct the RPT and SET Receivables balance and deferred RPT/SET Income as of December 31, 2018; and

The Municipal Treasurer, based on AR prepared by the Municipal Assessor, immediately update the RPTAR/TIC and then the Certified List of Taxpayers (CLT) showing real property taxes due and collectible for the year; and then submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the setting-up of RPT and SET Receivables for ensuing year.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count and prepare/submit the RPCPPE for CY 2019 and every year thereafter and the same shall be reconciled with the books of accounts.

We recommended the following:
1.	Preventive control or segregation of duties must be observe not only in the operation of slaughterhouse but in the whole operation of the Municipality;
2.	Immediately assign personnel other than the Collector to be in-charged in the assessment of slaughter fees.  Official Receipts must be supported with Order of Payment to ensure that every slaughter activity is properly assessed and receipted; and
3.	Assign personnel to check/investigate the previous slaughterhouse transactions to ensure that there was no loss of government funds and if found that there was mishandling and misuse of same, file an appropriate charges.

We recommended that the PRAISE Committee submits the employees’ individual accomplishment/appraisal or evaluation reports or employees’ contribution in compliance with the provisions of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2001.  Likewise, Municipality’s PRAISE Committee must have a written established guidelines in assessing the performance and conduct of each employee.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant remit regularly and within the prescribed period all the withheld trust funds due to the BIR.  Verify in the municipality’s book of accounts the correctness of prior year’s past due accounts with BIR and cause the proper adjusting entries, if found some error in recording.

We recommended that management (i) minimize or stop the practice of serving meals and snacks for entertaining guests or visitors in the normal course of conducting official functions and during ordinary staff meetings or conferences in order to prevent the incurrence of irregular an unnecessary expenditures and wastage of government funds; (ii) ensure that expenses to be reimbursed pass the “public purpose test” by requiring that the basic supporting documents for government expenditures are attached to the claims; and (iii) explain why these transactions will not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that:
The Municipal Accountant adjust the previously recorded RPT and SET Receivable based on the Certified List of Delinquent Accounts, to correct the RPT and SET Receivables balance and deferred RPT/SET Income as of December 31, 2018; and
The Municipal Treasurer, based on AR prepared by the Municipal Assessor, immediately update the RPTAR/TIC and then the Certified List of Taxpayers (CLT) showing real property taxes due and collectible for the year; and then submit the same to the Municipal Accountant as basis in the setting-up of RPT and SET Receivables for ensuing year.

We recommended that the agency develop and maintain a Gender and Development data base for gender statistics and sex-disaggregated data for planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person submit a GPB that conforms to the required format of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No 2016-01.

We recommended that the GAD Focal Person subject the attributed projects to the HGDG test in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No 2016-01.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the above stated report within the deadline set as required by the above mentioned Circular.

Management to strictly enforce settlement by persons’ liable of the suspension within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 82 of PD 1445; and
Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting documents before signing the DVs and to monitor settlement of NS to avoid accumulation of unsettled suspensions or disallowances.

We recommended that an Inspection Committee should be constituted to determine and classify the unserviceable properties. The valueless should be destroyed in the presence of the Auditor under the supervision of the proper committee or if found valuable, it should be disposed of on the manner provided under Section 379 of R. A. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipality secure the approval of the DILG Secretary for the procurement of the Motor Vehicle to avoid suspension and stop using the vehicle for purposes other than that required by DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management require the Municipal Treasurer to:
 Strictly observe daily recording of collections and deposits in the cashbooks;

Reconcile the cashbooks against the cash on hand on a daily basis for accuracy of balance at any given time; and

Deposit intact all collections with the authorized depository bank daily, or not later than the next banking day, to ensure protection from possible misuse of government funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the PRAISE Committee submit the employee’s individual accomplishment/appraisal or evaluation reports or employee’s contribution in compliance with the provisions of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2001.  Likewise, Municipality’s PRAISE Committee must have a written established guidelines in assessing the performance and conduct of each employee.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part 2 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Inventory Committee to come up with the Report on
   the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) in CY 2017 and every  year  thereafter
   and the same shall be reconciled with the books of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create a plantilla position
   and appoint a GSO or else designate a GSO who would also  be  responsible
   for supply and property management of the municipality.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to demand
   liquidation of cash advances and not to allow additional cash  advance(s)
   to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given him is
   first settled and accounted for in the books.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to:

   a. Impose the daily turn-over of collections  by  the  Revenue  Officers/
      collectors to the Municipal Treasurer;
   b. Strictly observe daily recording of collections and  deposits  in  the
      cashbooks;
   c. Reconcile the cashbooks against the cash on hand on a daily basis  for
      accuracy of balance at any given time; and
   d. Deposit intact all collections with  the  authorized  depository  bank
      daily, or not later than the next banking day,  to  ensure  protection
      from possible misuse of government funds.

a.  The  Local  Finance  Committee  make  a  systematic   and   realistic
      forecasting of revenue taking into account prior years’ experience and
      statistics  as  aids  in  setting  targets.   Review  of  the  present
      budgeting done under the stewardship of the  Municipal  Budget  Office
      should be undertaken to set  a  clearer  guide  per  item  of  revenue
      account for the Municipal Treasurer’s  Office  towards  attaining  the
      targets set;


   b. The Municipal Treasurer make  a  periodic  review  whether  collection
      targets are attained.  Conduct periodic review of  accomplishments  in
      order to have  assurance  that  all  revenue  sources  are  tapped  to
      maximize the revenue generating powers mandated by  law.  The  reasons
      for the shortfalls in collections should be analyzed  and  alternative
      actions should be looked into to avoid incurrence of shortfall in  the
      future;


   c. The Municipal Accountant make a proper adjusting entries in the  books
      of accounts the collections on “ante and post mortem  inspection  fee”
      for a total amount of P412,178.00 which were erroneously  recorded  in
      the Trust Fund; and


   d. The Treasury and Accounting Departments accomplish Quarterly Report of
      Income (LBAc Form No. 1) on periodic basis so as to keep track of  the
      status of revenue generation and at the same time adopt measures  that
      will likely improve collections for accounts not reaching the targets.
       This will provide the assurance that more plans and programs will  be
      fully implemented.

We recommended that  the  Local  Chief  Executive  to  intensify  further
   awareness of the constituents  thru  creative  information  campaign  and
   education activities in schools, barangays, commercial establishments and
   government offices on the “no segregation, no collection policy  and  the
   “no littering policy”.  Also, strictly enforce the sanctions to violators
   of the rules and regulations on ecological solid waste management.

We recommended that the Municipality prioritize only  programs,  projects
   and activities identified in the LDRRMFIP  that  are  chargeable  to  the
   DRRMF in accordance to the  provisions  of  COA  Circular  No.  2012-002,
   NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 2013-001 and  NDRRMC,  DILG,  DBM
   and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.

We recommended that the Municipality secure  the  approval  of  the  DILG
   Secretary for the procurement of the Motor Vehicle  to  avoid  suspension
   and stop using the vehicle for purposes other than that required by  DILG
   Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155.

We recommended that the LCPC prepare the Council’s Annual Work and
   Financial  Plan,  Local  Development  Plan  for  Children  (LDPC),  Local
   Investment Plan for Children (LIPC), Local Code for Children (LCC), Local
   State of Children (LSCR) and Children’s Profile in compliance to Section
   II.1 of DILG Memorandum Circulars No.  2012-120  and  to  utilize  their
   appropriated LCPC Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal  Accountant  submit  the  above  stated
   report within the  deadline  set  as  required  by  the  above  mentioned
   Circular.

We recommended that the  Local  School  Board  stop  the  charges  and
   payment of expenditures for the operation  of  the  district  office  and
   schools which are not directly in  line  with  the  PPAs  provided  under
   Section 4 of DepEd-DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 1 s. 2017.

We recommended  that  the  GAD  Focal  Person  submit  the  required  GAD
   checklist that is in compliance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA  Joint  Memorandum
   Circular No 2016-01 and to maximize the utilization of the GAD Fund.

We  recommended  that  Management  to  remit  regularly  and  within  the
   prescribed period all the withheld trust funds due to the BIR, GSIS, HDMF
   and PhilHealth.

We recommended that management to strictly enforce settlement by persons’
   liable of the suspension within the prescribed period pursuant to Section
   82 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the PRAISE Committee submits the employees individual
   accomplishment/appraisal or evaluation reports or employees  contribution
   in compliance with the provisions of CSC Memorandum  Circular  No.  1,  s.
   2001.  Likewise, Municipality’s PRAISE  Committee  must  have  a  written
   established guidelines in assessing the performance and conduct  of  each
   employee.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) monthly and the collectors to prepare the RAAF for verification not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.

We recommended that an Inspection Committee should be constituted to determine and classify the unserviceable properties. The valueless should be destroyed in the presence of the Auditor under the supervision of the proper committee or if found valuable, it should be disposed of on the manner provided under Section 379 of R. A. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The designated Property Custodian started inventory of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended processing of the transfer of titles of the land to the name of the Municipality of Ibajay, Aklan be fast-tracked so that the LGU’s ownership to the properties would be absolute and established for all legal intents and purposes.  Failure on the part of the LGU management to immediately effect the actual transfer may result to possible questions or issues regarding ownership in the future.
We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Planning and Dev’t Coordinator see to it that the 20% Development Fund are fully implemented in a timely manner.
We recommend that Local School Board should stop the charging of payment of services not related to the establishment of extension classes against the SEF.
observations.managementAction: DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 s. 2017 now allows the charging of Local School Board Teachers to SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommend therefore, that the agency collect sex-disaggregated data and gender related information on the involvement of men and women during project preparation and develop and maintain a GAD Database where the collected data will be contained necessary for the planning, programming and policy formulation to address gender issues.
observations.managementAction: Management started to establish a GAD database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management to strictly enforce settlement by persons’ liable of the suspension within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 82 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction:
Management was able to settle P283,332.46 out of the P644,398.76 suspended transactions.

Reiterated in Part 2 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Inventory Committee   to come up with the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) in CY 2016 and every year thereafter and the same shall be reconciled with the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction:
Physical Inventory of PPE is partially implemented and no RCPPE was prepared.

Reiterated in Part 2 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to intensify further awareness of the constituents thru creative information campaign and education activities in schools, barangays, commercial establishments and government offices on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no littering policy”.  Also, strictly enforce the sanctions to violators of the rules and regulations on ecological solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part 2 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the municipality stop charges of the above mentioned expenses to the Special Education Fund and strictly limit same only to those enumerated under Section 1 of RA 5447. Management should also submit a valid justification regarding disbursements not aligned with the purpose of the Special Education Fund.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part 2 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Engineer must see to it that responsibilities in performing works and functions in delivering construction and engineering services must be given to regular employees whose conduct and performance are under the direct control and supervision of the his office.

We recommended that management strictly comply with Section 36 of the Republic Act No. 9710 in order to recognize the role of women in nation building and ensure the fundamental gender equality.  Also, justify the disbursements not in line with GAD budget.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation of cash advances and not to allow additional cash advance(s) to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given him is first settled and accounted for in the books.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part 2 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that (1) the management stop using LDRMMF funds for program/activities that are not identified priority projects under the DRRM Plan; (2) management submit valid justification on the charging of P1.426 million on LDRRMF; and (3) the Municipal Accountant submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA Auditor on the prescribed date.
observations.managementAction: Reiterated in Part 2 of this AAR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit the Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) monthly and the collectors to prepare the RAAF for verification not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.
observations.managementAction: The Mun. Treasurer assigned personnel to prepare the report but still failed to submit the monthly report to the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU officials strictly adhere and comply with the basic rules in the utilization of fuel of government vehicles as provided for in Section V of COA Circular No.  75-6    November 7, 1975.
observations.managementAction: The municipality failed to submit monthly fuel consumption report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Local School Board should: (a) stop the charging of payment of services not related to the establishment of extension classes against the SEF; and (b) ensure that SEF be expended solely and exclusively for expenditures provided for under Joint Circular No. 01, s. 1998 issued by DECS, DBM and DILG dated April 14, 1998.
observations.managementAction:
Payment of services not related to SEF continuous to be charged against the Fund.

Audit observation and recommendation reiterated in part 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Inventory Committee   to come up with the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) in CY 2016 and every year thereafter and the same shall be reconciled with the books of accounts.
Also, require the issuance of an Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for all procured or acquired properties which shall be used to report on the receipt of property and equipment for official use from the Designated Property Officer.

We recommended that the PRAISE Committee submits the employees individual accomplishment/appraisal or evaluation reports or employees contribution in compliance with the provisions of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 1, s. 2001.  Likewise, Municipality’s PRAISE Committee must have a written established guidelines in assessing the performance and conduct of each employee.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to intensify further awareness of the constituents thru creative information campaign and education activities in schools, barangays, commercial establishments and government offices on the “no segregation, no collection policy and the “no littering policy”.  Also, strictly enforce the sanctions to violators of the rules and regulations on ecological solid waste management.

We recommended that the municipality stop charges of the above mentioned expenses to the Special Education Fund and strictly limit same only to those enumerated under Section 1 of RA 5447. Management should also submit a valid justification regarding disbursements not aligned with the purpose of the Special Education Fund.

We recommend that the Municipal Engineer must see to it that responsibilities in performing works and functions in delivering construction and engineering services must be given to regular employees whoseconduct and performance are under the direct control and supervision of the his office.

We recommended that management strictly comply with Section 36 of the Republic Act No. 9710 in order to recognize the role of women in nation building and ensure the fundamental gender equality.  Also, justify the disbursements not in line with GAD budget.
We recommended that management to strictly enforce settlement by persons’ liable of the suspension within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 82 of PD 1445.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Accountant to demand liquidation of cash advances and not to allow additional cash advance(s) to any official or employee unless the previous cash advance given him is first settled and accounted for in the books.

We recommend that (1) the management stop using LDRMMF funds for program/activities that are not identified priority projects under the DRRM Plan; (2) management submit valid justification on the charging of P1.426 million on LDRRMF; and (3) the Municipal Accountant submit Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF to COA Auditor on the prescribed date.

We recommended that management require the immediate liquidation of cash advances within the time frame required by law. The municipal accountant should monitor the liquidation of cash advances by sending demand letters regularly and the Municipal Treasurer to withhold payment of any money due them in case of their failure to settle the liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: The amount of Cash Advances as of year end has significantly decreased as compared to last year, and the remaining unliquidated advances were immediately liquidated within the first month of the preceding year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the local Chief Executive  create an Inventory Committee to undertake the physical inventory of all the PPE of the municipality. Likewise, we recommend that management should prepare and submit the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment as required in Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I.
observations.managementAction:
Physical Inventory of PPE is partially implemented and no RCPPE was prepared.

Audit observation and recommendation reiterated in part 2 of this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that an Inspection Committee should be constituted to determine and classify the unserviceable properties. The valueless should be destroyed in the presence of the Auditor under the supervision of the proper committee or if found valuable, it should be disposed of on the manner provided under Section 379 of R. A. 7160.
observations.managementAction: The designated Property Custodian started inventory of unserviceable properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended processing of the transfer of titles of the land to the name of the Municipality of Ibajay, Aklan be fast-tracked so that the LGU’s ownership to the properties would be absolute and established for all legal intents and purposes.  Failure on the part of the LGU management to immediately effect the actual transfer may result to possible questions or issues regarding ownership in the future.
observations.managementAction: The documents are still in the Municipal Assessors Office for processing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Office of the Municipal Planning and Dev’t  Coordinator see to it that the 20% Development Fund are fully implemented in a timely manner.
observations.managementAction: During the year, the municipality had utilized 74.84 percent of the 20 percent of DF appropriation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the management require the Municipal Treasurer to:

 Strictly observe daily recording of collections and deposits in the cashbooks;

Reconcile the cashbooks against the cash on hand on a daily basis for accuracy of balance at any given time; and

Deposit intact all collections with the authorized depository bank daily, or not later than the next banking day, to ensure protection from possible misuse of government funds.
observations.managementAction: During Cash Examination, we have noted that cashbooks were updated, except for the Cash in Bank General Fund – IRA cashbook. Intact deposit of collections on the next banking day remain unobserved.

observations.recommendation: We recommend therefore, that the agency collect sex-disaggregated data and gender related information on the involvement of men and women during project preparation and develop and maintain a GAD Database where the collected data will be contained necessary for the planning, programming and policy formulation to address gender issues.
observations.managementAction: Management started to establish a GAD database.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly enforce settlement by persons’ liable of the suspension within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 82 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction:
Management was able to settle P847,206.37 of the total P1,491,605.13 suspended transactions

Audit observation and recommendation reiterated in part 2 of this report.
